reasoning: Q3 is a 3-way join (customerâ†’ordersâ†’lineitem, 1.5Mâ†’15Mâ†’60M rows) with 20% selective filter on customer and date filters on orders/lineitem. Current 386ms vs DuckDB 78ms (5x gap) indicates significant optimization potential. Primary levers: 64-core parallelism, optimal join ordering (dimension pushdown), zone map pruning for date filters, partial aggregation, and Top-K heap instead of full sort.
focus_areas: Parallel hash joins across 64 threads with morsel-driven execution (10K-50K row morsels)

Join sequence: filter customer (c_mktsegment='BUILDING', 20% selectivity) â†’ build hash table â†’ probe with orders (filter o_orderdate < '1995-03-15') â†’ probe with lineitem (filter l_shipdate > '1995-03-15')
Exploit zone maps on o_orderdate and l_shipdate to skip blocks (lineitem sorted by l_shipdate, orders sorted by o_orderdate)
Partial aggregation: thread-local hash tables for GROUP BY (l_orderkey, o_orderdate, o_shippriority), then merge
Top-K heap (size 10) instead of full sort on ~300K groups â€” maintain min-heap during aggregation
SIMD (AVX512) for arithmetic expression: l_extendedprice * (1 - l_discount) â€” process 8 int64 values per instruction
Use existing indexes: custkey_hash on customer/orders, orderkey_hash on orders, orderkey_sorted on lineitem
strategy_notes: This is a dimension-pushdown join pattern. The critical path is: (1) Filter customer early (reduces 1.5Mâ†’300K rows), (2) Hash join with orders using zone map pruning on dates, (3) Hash join with lineitem using zone map pruning. Parallelize all scans and joins with morsel-driven execution. Use partial aggregation to avoid contention on global hash table. Top-K heap optimization is crucial â€” with 300K groups and LIMIT 10, full sort is wasteful. SIMD for aggregation arithmetic. Expected performance: 2-3x speedup from parallelism + zone maps, 1.5x from Top-K heap, 1.2x from SIMD â†’ target ~60-80ms (competitive with DuckDB).
notes: Focus on getting parallelism right first â€” this is the primary lever with 64 cores. Zone maps on date columns should provide significant block skipping. The Top-K heap optimization is high-impact (avoid sorting 300K groups when only 10 are needed). Ensure thread-local partial aggregation to avoid lock contention.
